model_narrative: We run a ridesharing service that must decide which vehicle serves which ride request and along which route. Each request can only be served by a limited set of eligible vehicles, and for any served request exactly one vehicle on one route is chosen. Each possible vehicle-request-route assignment carries a cost, and using the road network over the planning horizon adds a congestion-related cost on top. The objective is to choose the assignments that make the combined assignment and congestion cost as small as possible.

sets.name: R
sets.doc: the ride requests, each one a passenger trip that may or may not be served

sets.name: V
sets.doc: the vehicles available in the fleet

sets.name: paths
sets.doc: the alternative route options a vehicle can take to serve a request

sets.name: L
sets.doc: the road links in the network; used only by the objective, not by the constraints

sets.name: h
sets.doc: the planning horizon time steps; used only by the objective, not by the constraints

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the dynamic-network-loading time steps; used only by the objective, not by the constraints

params.name: Vr
params.doc: for each request, the list of vehicles that are eligible to serve that request; only these vehicle-request pairings are allowed, all other vehicles cannot be assigned to that request

params.name: beta
params.doc: the assignment cost of having a vehicle serve a request on a given route; used only by the objective

params.name: Cl
params.doc: the capacity of each road link; used only by the objective

params.name: rho
params.doc: a per-link, per-time-step congestion ratio between zero and one; used only by the objective

params.name: q
params.doc: a per-link, per-time-step flow figure; used only by the objective

params.name: n
params.doc: a per-link, per-dynamic-loading-step figure; used only by the objective

params.name: p
params.doc: a fixed count parameter; equal to 20 in this instance

params.name: m
params.doc: a fixed count parameter; equal to 8 in this instance. The smaller of p and m sets the exact number of assignments to make, which here is 8

vars.name: x
vars.doc: one if the vehicle is assigned to serve the request on that route, zero otherwise

vars.name: alpha
vars.doc: a link-usage indicator for an assignment over time; appears only in the objective, never in the constraints

description: Each ride request can be served at most once. For every request, look across all of its eligible vehicles and all available routes, and make sure that at most one of those vehicle and route assignments is chosen for that request.

Each possible vehicle-request-route assignment carries a cost, and using the road network over the planning horizon adds a congestion-related cost on top. The objective is to choose the assignments that make the combined assignment and congestion cost as small as possible.

params.name: m

vars.name: x

vars.name: alpha

description: The service must make a fixed total number of assignments across the whole operation. Adding up every chosen vehicle, request, and route assignment over all requests and their eligible vehicles, the total number of assignments made must equal the smaller of the two fixed count parameters.
expected_pyomo:
def eq_12_rule(model):
    min_const = min(model.p.value, model.m.value)
    return sum(model.x[v, r, a] for r in model.R for v in model.Vr[r].value for a in model.paths) == min_const
model.eq_12 = Constraint(rule=eq_12_rule)

params.name: Cl

params.name: rho

vars.name: alpha
vars.doc: a link-usage indicator for an assignment over time; appears only in the objective, never in the constraints

description: To build the complete model, enforce the following relationships in order. First, each ride request can be served at most once. For every request, look across all of its eligible vehicles and all available routes, and make sure that at most one of those vehicle and route assignments is chosen for that request. Finally, the service must make a fixed total number of assignments across the whole operation. Adding up every chosen vehicle, request, and route assignment over all requests and their eligible vehicles, the total number of assignments made must equal the smaller of the two fixed count parameters.
expected_pyomo:
def eq_10_rule(model, r):
    return sum(model.x[v, r, a] for v in model.Vr[r].value for a in model.paths) <= 1
model.eq_10 = Constraint(model.R, rule=eq_10_rule)
def eq_12_rule(model):
    min_const = min(model.p.value, model.m.value)
    return sum(model.x[v, r, a] for r in model.R for v in model.Vr[r].value for a in model.paths) == min_const
model.eq_12 = Constraint(rule=eq_12_rule)
